title: Yuki's Bouncy Ball Laboratory
synopsis: When Yuki's favorite bouncy ball won't stop bouncing, she turns her bedroom into a science laboratory to discover why some balls bounce high and others don't. With help from her diverse group of friends, Yuki conducts hilarious experiments that teach her about gravity, materials, and the joy of scientific discovery.

pages.text: Yuki loved her new bouncy ball. It was bright purple with sparkly stars. She threw it down hard in her bedroom. BOING! The ball bounced up and hit the ceiling. BONK! It bounced off the wall. THUMP! It knocked over her toy dinosaur.

illustration.description: A young Japanese girl with short black hair in pigtails, wearing a bright yellow t-shirt and blue overalls, looking surprised as a sparkly purple ball bounces wildly around her colorful bedroom. The ball is mid-bounce near the ceiling, with a green toy dinosaur falling over on a wooden shelf. Rich saturated colors with a whimsical, playful mood.
illustration.alt: Yuki watching her bouncy ball bounce wildly in her bedroom

pages.text: "Whoa!" said Yuki. "This ball is super bouncy!" The ball kept bouncing. It bounced off her bed. It bounced off her bookshelf. It bounced and bounced and bounced. "Why won't it stop?" Yuki wondered. She tried to catch it, but it was too fast.

illustration.description: Yuki running around her messy bedroom trying to catch the purple bouncy ball, her arms outstretched and eyes wide with determination. Books are scattered on the floor, her bed covers are rumpled, and the ball is shown as a purple blur bouncing between furniture. Bright, energetic colors with motion lines showing the ball's crazy path.
illustration.alt: Yuki chasing her bouncy ball around her messy room

pages.text: Finally, the ball rolled under her bed and stopped. Yuki got down on her hands and knees. She pulled out the ball and three other balls too. There was a red rubber ball, a blue tennis ball, and a soft yellow foam ball. "Hmm," said Yuki. "I wonder which one bounces the highest!"

illustration.description: Yuki kneeling beside her bed, holding four different colored balls - purple sparkly, red rubber, blue tennis ball, and soft yellow foam ball. She has a curious, thoughtful expression with her finger on her chin. The bedroom setting shows dust bunnies under the bed and warm lighting. Expressive big eyes showing wonder and curiosity.
illustration.alt: Yuki discovering different balls under her bed

pages.text: Yuki had an idea. "I'll be a scientist!" she said. She put on her mom's safety goggles and her dad's lab coat. The coat was way too big, but she felt very scientific. Her friends Marcus and Priya came over to play. "What are you doing?" asked Marcus, a boy with curly brown hair.

illustration.description: Yuki wearing oversized safety goggles and a white lab coat that's comically too big for her, standing proudly in her bedroom. Marcus, a Black boy with curly brown hair wearing a green striped shirt, and Priya, a South Asian girl with long black braids wearing a pink dress, are looking at her with amused expressions. Bright, cheerful colors with a playful laboratory setup in the background.
illustration.alt: Yuki dressed as a scientist with her friends Marcus and Priya

pages.text: "I'm doing a bounce experiment!" Yuki announced. "We're going to test which ball bounces highest." Priya clapped her hands. "That sounds fun! Can I help?" she asked. "Of course!" said Yuki. "Marcus, you can be the ball-dropper. Priya, you can be the bounce-measurer. I'll be the head scientist!"

illustration.description: The three children standing in a circle, with Yuki still in her oversized lab coat gesturing excitedly. Marcus is holding one of the balls, and Priya has a big smile with her hands clapped together. The bedroom has been transformed into a makeshift laboratory with the four different balls lined up on the floor. Bright, enthusiastic expressions with rich saturated colors.
illustration.alt: The three friends planning their bounce experiment together

pages.text: They lined up the balls. Marcus dropped the soft yellow foam ball first. PLOP! It barely bounced at all. "That's boring," giggled Priya. Next came the red rubber ball. It bounced up to Marcus's knee. "Better!" said Yuki, making notes on her pretend clipboard. The blue tennis ball bounced up to his waist. "Even better!" she cheered.

illustration.description: Marcus dropping balls while the three friends watch intently, with motion lines showing the different bounce heights - the yellow foam ball staying low, the red ball reaching knee height, and the blue tennis ball bouncing to waist level. Yuki is holding a cardboard clipboard, taking notes. The scene shows scientific curiosity and excitement with vibrant colors and expressive faces.
illustration.alt: The friends testing how high different balls bounce

pages.text: Finally, Marcus dropped the purple sparkly ball. BOING! It bounced higher than his head! It bounced so high it almost hit the ceiling again. "Wow!" said all three friends together. "The sparkly ball wins!" announced Yuki. But she still wondered why. "What makes it bounce so high?" she asked.

illustration.description: All three children looking up in amazement as the purple sparkly ball bounces incredibly high above their heads, almost reaching the ceiling. Their faces show wonder and excitement with big eyes and open mouths. The ball has sparkly trail effects as it bounces. Warm, bright lighting emphasizes the magical feeling of discovery.
illustration.alt: The friends amazed by how high the purple ball bounces

pages.text: Priya had an idea. "Let's squeeze all the balls!" she said. They squeezed each ball carefully. The foam ball was very soft and squishy. The rubber ball was medium-soft. The tennis ball was a little hard. But the sparkly ball was super hard! "I think hard balls bounce higher!" said Priya. "Great observation!" said Yuki.

illustration.description: The three friends sitting in a circle on the bedroom floor, each squeezing different balls to test their firmness. Their expressions show concentration and discovery. Yuki is squeezing the hard sparkly ball, Marcus has the soft foam ball, and Priya is testing the rubber ball. Scientific curiosity radiates from their focused faces with bright, engaging colors.
illustration.alt: The friends testing how hard or soft each ball feels

pages.text: "We're real scientists now!" said Yuki proudly. "We discovered that harder balls bounce higher than softer balls!" Marcus nodded. "And we learned that some balls barely bounce at all." "Science is fun!" laughed Priya. They played with all the balls for the rest of the day, bouncing them in different ways and making new discoveries. Yuki couldn't wait to do more experiments!

illustration.description: All three friends celebrating their scientific discovery, with Yuki taking off her oversized goggles and raising her arms in triumph. Marcus and Priya are laughing and clapping. The four balls are scattered around them on the floor, and there's a sense of accomplishment and joy. Golden afternoon light streams through the window, creating a warm, successful conclusion to their experiment.
illustration.alt: The three friends celebrating their successful science experiment

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What color was Yuki's favorite bouncy ball?
questions.explanation: Yuki's favorite ball was bright purple with sparkly stars, and it was the bounciest of all!

questions.question: Which ball bounced the lowest in their experiment?
questions.explanation: The soft yellow foam ball barely bounced at all when Marcus dropped it - it just went 'PLOP!'

questions.question: What did the friends discover about bouncing balls?
questions.explanation: The friends discovered that harder balls bounce higher than softer balls by squeezing and testing each one!

questions.question: Who were Yuki's two friends who helped with the experiment?
questions.explanation: Marcus and Priya came over to help Yuki with her bouncing ball experiment. Marcus was the ball-dropper and Priya was the bounce-measurer!

questions.question: What did Yuki wear to look like a scientist?
choices: A lab coat and safety goggles
questions.explanation: Yuki put on her mom's safety goggles and her dad's lab coat, which was way too big but made her feel very scientific!

pages.title: Fill in the Bouncy Words!
pages.instructions: Help Yuki complete her science notes by choosing the right words!
pages.text: Today I learned that {0} balls bounce higher than {1} balls. My {2} ball bounced the highest because it was very hard. The {3} ball barely bounced because it was too soft. Science experiments are {4}!

pages.title: Bounce Height Challenge!
pages.instructions: Put the balls in order from lowest bounce to highest bounce!
pages.text: Help Yuki remember which balls bounced highest! The {0} foam ball bounced lowest, the {1} rubber ball bounced to Marcus's knee, the {2} tennis ball bounced to his waist, and the {3} sparkly ball bounced highest of all!
